name: Peaches
description: Peach flesh (Prunus persica) is not toxic to cats in small amounts, but the pit, seeds, stems, and leaves contain amygdalin — a cyanogenic glycoside that releases hydrogen cyanide (HCN) when chewed or metabolised. This is the same compound found in apricot, cherry, and plum pits. The flesh alone is safe as an occasional treat.
safetyNotes: Pit, seeds, leaves, stems: toxic — amygdalin (cyanogenic glycoside) releases HCN via beta-glucosidase
Symptoms from pit ingestion: rapid breathing, weakness, drooling, vomiting; severe cases cause convulsions and respiratory failure
Ripe peach flesh: safe in small amounts, but high in sugar — not nutritionally beneficial for cats
Never give a whole peach — remove the pit completely before offering any flesh
Canned or sweetened peach products should be avoided due to added sugar or xylitol

symptoms.name: The symptoms a cat shows after ingesting peaches depend heavily on which part of the fruit was consumed. While the flesh of a peach is not toxic
Diarrhea and Loose Stools
symptoms.onset: Shortly after ingestion (within a few hours).
symptoms.notes: While the fleshy part of the peach is not chemically poisonous, it can still irritate a cat's digestive tract and lead to mild gastrointestinal upset

symptoms.name: Hyperventilation and Difficulty Breathing
symptoms.onset: Rapid onset (often within minutes to a few hours after chewing a pit).
symptoms.notes: If a cat chews or crushes the peach pit, the released cyanide binds to cellular enzymes and halts cellular oxygen metabolism12. This effectively suffocates the tissues, forcing the cat to hyperventilate as the body struggles to utilize oxygen

symptoms.name: Shock, Collapse, and Coma
symptoms.notes: Cyanide is an extremely potent toxin that rapidly starves vital organs (like the brain and heart) of energy, resulting in abnormally low blood pressure, shock, loss of consciousness, coma, and ultimately death if untreated

symptoms.name: Neurological Dysfunction (Dizziness, Ataxia, and Confusion)
symptoms.notes: The sudden lack of cellular energy production caused by cyanide poisoning quickly damages nerves and impairs central nervous system function, leading to dizziness, mental confusion, and an inability to walk properly

symptoms.name: Vomiting and Nausea
symptoms.onset: Shortly after ingestion (within 30 minutes to a few hours).
symptoms.notes: Vomiting can occur either as an initial reaction to the cyanide toxicity from the pit or simply as a response to gastrointestinal irritation

symptoms.name: Cherry-Red Skin or Mucous Membranes
symptoms.notes: Because cyanide blocks the cells from extracting oxygen from the bloodstream, the venous blood remains fully oxygenated. This clinical hallmark causes the cat's skin, gums, and mucous membranes to turn an abnormally bright, cherry-red color

symptoms.name: Gastrointestinal or Throat Obstruction (Physical Hazard)
symptoms.onset: Immediate (if lodged in the throat) or variable (taking hours to days if lodged in the intestinal tr
symptoms.notes: Aside from the chemical dangers of a crushed seed, swallowing an intact, hard peach pit poses a major mechanical hazard2. The pit can easily become lodged in the cat's throat, causing immediate choking, or block the intestinal tract, requiring emergency surgical removal
